notes: Earthman Chronicles · the six chapters of the game's playable structure.  Each chapter has: title, setting, sub-zones (each sub-zone is a first-person grid or tactical scene), critical-path beats (linear story progression), branch beats (player-choice inflections), boss encounters, key NPCs met, Workings available, Corrections findable.  Manuscript-vs-game comparison: manuscript has 12 chapters, game compresses to 6.  Astro-Cortex's compression decisions matter · what was cut, what was condensed, what was expanded.

schema_notes.sub_zones: cells/locations within the chapter · each is a distinct explorable space
schema_notes.branches: player-choice moments that fork or gate content
schema_notes.workings_available: which Thelemic Workings can be performed during this chapter
schema_notes.corrections_findable: which of the six Corrections can be discovered in this chapter

chapters.title: Pasadena · 1946
chapters.setting: January 17, 1946.  Jack Whiteside's home laboratory in the coach-house at 1071 South Orange Grove Boulevard, Pasadena, California.  A rainy Thursday evening.  Two hours before the ritual begins.
chapters.tone: cold indoors · warm laboratory light · specific 1946 California mundanity punctuated by the imminent.  The player is in Jack's actual life for the last time.

sub_zones.features: Jack's chemistry bench · the propellant compound is here · three white candles in a drawer · the specific old book on a specific high shelf · a specific spectrum analyzer Jack built himself (this is the item that visualizes 220 Hz later)
sub_zones.notable: the coach-house workshop's north window looks out on the San Gabriels · a specific still image · this is the last thing Jack sees before Parsa

sub_zones.features: Jack's personal library · Crowley volumes · Ordo Templi Orientis pamphlets · a filing cabinet with the OTO Contract in it (findable in Chapter 3, but visible here) · a specific photograph on the desk of a woman labeled 'HELEN 1942' who is not Sara
sub_zones.notable: reading specific books here grants specific dialogue-tree unlocks later

sub_zones.features: specific 1946 kitchen · a coffee percolator · a specific loaf of bread · Jack can eat here (delays the ritual by one hour · gives +5 SP) · a specific unopened letter from his mother
sub_zones.notable: the letter from his mother is the only place in the game she is mentioned · reading it is optional but unlocks a specific Chapter 6 dialogue

sub_zones.features: Jack's bed · a specific nightstand · a specific book he's currently reading · a specific medicine (contains a specific 1946 barbiturate · picking it up is a choice that has narrative consequences)
sub_zones.notable: sleeping in the bed advances to the ritual · not sleeping means Jack is tired at the ritual · specific difficulty implication

sub_zones.features: the actual room where the ritual occurs · specific chalk marks on the floor · a specific altar · the four cardinal points marked · Rafaton is waiting here
sub_zones.notable: the arc-based chalk-drawing minigame occurs here · a specific rhythm minigame follows

critical_path: Jack wakes in his bedroom · reads book briefly · specific establishing dialogue with the audience
Jack collects ritual components across the coach-house · propellant compound, candles, old book, bowl
Rafaton arrives at the door · Jack lets him in · specific establishing dialogue
Jack chalks the ritual sigil (arc-based minigame · fail = re-chalk · frustrating = flavor)
The three options for the working manifest as menu (see branches below)
The ritual is performed (rhythm minigame · Rafaton keeps time)
The Working completes · SEE branches for outcome
Chapter ends with Jack on the desert of Parsa

branches.moment: before the ritual · menu · three options

choices.consequence: Jack meets a hostile Delvanni scout in Chapter 2 · combat opens Chapter 2
Jack meets Hel Velli in Chapter 2 · dialogue opens Chapter 2
Jack meets a Kelait child in Chapter 2 · different Chapter 2 pacing
Rafaton becomes a party-adjacent NPC in Chapter 3+ · his true role revealed EARLIER
Rafaton is absent Chapter 3-4 · returns Chapter 5 as expected reveal

workings_available: star_ruby (available AFTER chapter · Ch. 2 opens the Workings menu)

chapters.title: The Approach
chapters.setting: Parsa's outer wastelands.  Rust-red sand, two moons (one dying).  Jack wakes with no water and no bearings.
chapters.tone: vast · disorienting · genuinely wild.  The scale of Parsa is what hits.  Jack is small here.

sub_zones.features: the specific spot Jack landed · a specific rock formation nearby · a specific dune-cactus of note · a specific corpse of a Parsan creature
sub_zones.notable: the corpse is the first place Jack learns about Parsan biology · Sara Nai later comments on it (she reads about it later in a Kyrindi archive)

sub_zones.features: the specific dune Jack climbs · at the top he can see for the first time · Hel Velli is at the bottom of the dune · looking up
sub_zones.notable: specific establishing shot · the animation of Hel Velli looking up when Jack appears is authored

sub_zones.features: the Kel-Danari tribal camp · fifteen Delvanni present · a specific fire ring · a specific corral of Parsan mounts
sub_zones.notable: Jack's introduction to Delvanni culture · the tribal circle etiquette must be learned · a specific first-taste-of-food ceremony

sub_zones.features: the duel arena · a specific packed-dirt circle · Murg of the Kel-Karai · a specific weapons rack
sub_zones.notable: the briefcase joke option is here · combat gameplay properly introduced

critical_path: Jack wakes on Parsa · initial exploration · learns temperature/thirst basics
Meets Hel Velli · initial dialogue (respect must be earned)
Follows Hel Velli to the Kel-Danari tribal circle
Tribal circle etiquette · a specific dialogue tree · succeeds or fails
Duel with Murg (opposing tribe's champion) · combat proper introduction
Duel resolution · joke option, respect earned, or beaten
Hel Velli joins the party formally
Chapter ends with the party departing toward Talikan

choices.consequence: the briefcase joke · Murg laughs · duel becomes friendly · Hel Velli's respect earned via humor
traditional duel · win or lose changes Hel Velli's respect direction
use Rafaton's coaching (if he crossed with Jack) · easy win but Hel Velli's respect drops

chapters.title: Talikan
chapters.setting: The Kyrindi capital.  A city of white marble and green-tinted glass, built into a specific mesa.  Two rivers meet here (both fresh, both cold, both drinkable).  Population approximately 140,000.  Sara Nai's home.
manuscript_chapters_covered: V. The Court of the Kyrindi
chapters.tone: civilized · elegant · specifically educated.  Jack is impressed and slightly out of his depth.  Sara Nai is at ease here in a way she is not later.

sub_zones.features: the specific main gate of Talikan · four Kyrindi guards · a specific inscription in Kyrindi that Jack cannot read (yet)
sub_zones.notable: the inscription is translated in a specific Chapter 3-late scene · reads: 'we welcome the specific stranger who knows what he does not know'

sub_zones.features: the main market · booths selling specific Kyrindi crafts · a specific Kelait musician (Yr's grandfather) · a specific bakery run by a specific Kyrindi widow · a specific gold-money-exchange booth
sub_zones.notable: Jack can buy specific items here · the Kelait musician plays a fragment of the mourning song (subconscious cross-Oneironautics tie)

sub_zones.features: the great library of Talikan · fifteen thousand Kelait ancient texts preserved here · a specific reading room · a specific Kyrindi scholar (Sara Nai's mentor, High Scribe Arentha)
sub_zones.notable: the OTO Contract is here in a specific hidden desk drawer marked with the eight-pointed star · Correction 3 unlockable

sub_zones.features: the royal palace · specific state rooms · King Vessel's throne room · Sara Nai's personal chambers (accessible after her permission)
sub_zones.notable: Sara Nai's introduction happens here · her chambers include her personal library · Kelait fragments she has been studying

sub_zones.features: a specific dedicated ritual space Sara Nai maintains · she offers it to Jack for his Workings
sub_zones.notable: Working I (Star Ruby) is performed here · Working IV (Hymn of Pan) available here

critical_path: Party arrives at Talikan · gate scene · Kyrindi guards let them pass (Hel Velli's tribal ID token gets them through)
Market exploration · Jack meets specific NPCs · finds specific items
Palace audience · Jack meets King Vessel · Sara Nai present but not formally introduced
Sara Nai deliberately encounters Jack in the library later that night · SHE has arranged the meeting · this is a specific reveal
First Working (Star Ruby) performed in Sara's ritual chamber · Sara Nai formally joins the party
Chapter 3-mid · the Delvanni conflict news arrives · a specific tribal war is being staged by an unknown party
Party investigates · discovers Kelait settlement in the crossfire · the Delvanni conflict is being STAGED to raid the Kelait
Chapter ends with the party planning to descend into the mines to free the Kelait

choices.consequence: Jack acknowledges Sara arranged the meeting · unlocks specific Sara dialogue where she admits she summoned him academically
Jack plays along with fate-narrative · Sara's disposition slightly lower · specific Chapter 5 dialog changes
Arentha shows Jack the OTO Contract · Correction 3 available
Arentha refers Jack to Mother Kanel · sets up Chapter 4 arrival
Arentha talks about Sara as scholar · unlocks her academic-collaboration dialogue tree

chapters.title: The Mines of Delvan
chapters.setting: The vast Delvanni mining operation of the Kel-Rethani tribe (not Hel Velli's Kel-Danari · a different tribe · larger, richer, more industrial).  Underground.  Extensive.  Home to approximately 800 enslaved Kelait.  Mother Kanel is here.  Yr is here.
chapters.tone: underground · claustrophobic · the tone shift is intentional.  The manuscript rendered this as mountain-monastery uplifting; the game renders it as industrial-slavery grim.  The change matters.

sub_zones.features: the outer approach · Delvanni guards · a specific bribable overseer named DALEV · a specific hidden Kelait signal-lantern
sub_zones.notable: the signal-lantern is the game's introduction to Kelait aphorism-as-cipher · Sara Nai can read it if the player has unlocked her Kelait tree

sub_zones.features: the main working level · Kelait mining ore under Delvanni supervision · specific dangerous chambers · a specific unattended smelting furnace
sub_zones.notable: Jack witnesses the enslavement here · Mother Kanel is not yet accessible

sub_zones.features: the enslaved Kelait have a specific kitchen where Mother Kanel presides · a specific pot on a specific fire · a specific salt jar next to a specific pot
sub_zones.notable: the aphorism 'the salt in the pot is not the same salt as in the sea' is literalized here · Jack can figure out it's a recipe · trust with Mother Kanel opens

sub_zones.features: the enslaved Kelait live here · Yr is here · a specific hidden library of Kelait texts (they've saved fragments over centuries)
sub_zones.notable: Yr's introduction happens here · specific Rocha-team emotional-work animation of him looking at a specific something

sub_zones.features: the Kelait's actual mystical practice space · never seen by Delvanni · a specific pool of underground water · a specific Kelait sage NPC (Mother Kanel's own teacher, still alive at 200+)
sub_zones.notable: the game's deepest Kelait wisdom is delivered here · Mother Kanel's mortality speech happens here · this is the emotional apex of Chapter 4

sub_zones.features: the Kel-Rethani overseer's chamber · the specific overseer (a Delvanni named THAR-KRAI-TAM · morally complex · has a family, has been doing this for years, is a real character not a monster)
sub_zones.notable: the Chapter 4 boss can be BEATEN or NEGOTIATED · both outcomes canonical · Rocha's team wrote a specific empathy dialogue tree with Thar-Krai-Tam that if pursued fully lets you free the Kelait without killing him

Descent through Level 1 · witness enslavement
Enter Kanel's Kitchen · initial mistrust · earn trust via the salt-pot puzzle
Mother Kanel opens · the Kelait aphorism-cipher explained
Party plans the escape · specific tactical decisions (who leaves first, how, when)
Meet Yr · establish emotional stakes
Descend to the Kelait secret · Mother Kanel's mortality speech
Working V (Mass of the Phoenix) available here IF the player chooses · Hel Velli refuses
Chapter 4 climax · confrontation with Thar-Krai-Tam · combat OR empathy path
Rescue succeeds (canonical if empathy path) OR partially succeeds (canonical if combat path with specific damage) OR fails (specific rare outcome · Yr dies · Chapter 6 references)
Rocha can be recruited here · specific dialogue: 'Are you SURE you're just a cartographer's assistant?'
Chapter ends with the party emerging from the mines, Yr in tow OR Yr not, depending on outcome

chapters.title: The Academy
chapters.setting: The ORDER OF THE PROPHET's central training facility · a specific mountain-set complex Jack is INVITED to.  The invitation comes from Rafaton via a specific Delvanni courier.  Jack arrives thinking this is his 'graduation.'  It is not.
chapters.tone: reveal · shift · the game's actual antagonist is unveiled here · the whole story recontextualizes.  Rocha's team labeled this chapter 'THE PIVOT' in production docs.

sub_zones.features: the mountain approach · specific Order-of-the-Prophet architecture (built in the last 200 years · stone with metal inlays · eight-pointed star motifs everywhere · not native to any Parsan species)
sub_zones.notable: Sara Nai's dialogue in this zone begins to shift · she recognizes architecture · she has been to a similar place · she does not tell Jack yet

sub_zones.features: the great entrance · Rafaton greets the party · a specific ceremonial welcome · food, wine, respect · everything comfortable · everything wrong
sub_zones.notable: the specific dialogue here is careful · Rafaton is being warm · warmth is uncanny · the player who is paying attention notices

sub_zones.features: a specific chamber where Jack meets the Scarlet Woman · velvet-draped · photoluminescent moss lighting · a specific tea service
sub_zones.notable: the three canonical readings dialogue tree happens here · player picks their interpretation

sub_zones.features: the Order's central meeting chamber · nine members visible · eight of them are Rafaton-adjacent figures · the ninth is RONSON
sub_zones.notable: the reveal happens here · Rafaton is revealed to be Nalat, senior Order member · Jack has been guided all along · Sara Nai's face is a specific animation the writers noted

sub_zones.features: Ronson's private study · specific Earth-manufactured objects (a specific California postcard, a specific 1930s book, a specific ashtray from a specific Los Angeles restaurant)
sub_zones.notable: Ronson is a Hubbard-analog · has been on Parsa 14 years · arrived via an earlier ritual · specific climactic soliloquy happens here

sub_zones.features: a hidden room accessible only via 220-Hz-marked scene · Jack's spectrum analyzer visualizes it · a specific closet · the Pasadena Fire Correction is here
sub_zones.notable: Correction 1 unlockable · requires Jack to have the spectrum analyzer from Chapter 1

sub_zones.features: the Chapter 5 boss encounter · Nalat-as-Rafaton · optionally supplemented by two lesser Order members · optionally Ronson if the player has provoked him
sub_zones.notable: combat OR negotiation · Nalat can be talked down if the player has the OTO Contract Correction and specific dialogue lines · a specific 'you have completed six Workings on my instruction, Jack. Do you not wonder why? Well. I get a specific bonus for each' line lands here

chapters.title: The Finale
chapters.setting: Depends on the ending path.  For REFUSED THE WORK · a Kyrindi city called TALIKAN or a Delvanni settlement or the deep Kelait wisdom cave.  For LIBERATOR · Kyrindi great hall at the coronation.  For BABALON COMES · an unspecified higher plane.  For RETURN TO EARTH · the Pasadena laboratory.  For THE CORRECTION · the Trailer's compendium.  For HUBBARD TAKES THE CREDIT · the Order's main chamber.

sub_zones.features: Kyrindi great hall · coronation · Sara Nai at Jack's side · Hel Velli standing behind · the Delvanni tribes gathered · Rafaton (if not defeated) whispers congratulations · Sara's crown is slightly too small · she adjusts it
sub_zones.notable: Ending 1 · THE WARLORD OF PARSA

sub_zones.features: Pasadena · January 20 1946 · Jack in his laboratory · holding a book · Sara Northrup on the porch · then 1947 · 1948 · 1949 · 1950 · 1951 · 1952 · then the specific 5:57 PM June 17 image
sub_zones.notable: Ending 2 · RETURN TO EARTH

sub_zones.features: Working IX completes · Sara Nai transforms and remains transformed · Los Angeles 1946 from above · new star in the sky · specific voice · specific closed door
sub_zones.notable: Ending 3 · BABALON COMES

sub_zones.features: Jack refuses Working IX · stays on Parsa · lives in Talikan · Sara Nai with him · Hel Velli visits · Kelait mourning song plays · specific montage · Jack dies of age at 70 · Sara mourns in untranslated Kyrindi · Mother Kanel appears one last time
sub_zones.notable: Ending 4 · REFUSED THE WORK · Rocha's team's preferred ending

sub_zones.features: the game breaks its own frame · Rocha's handwritten note scrolls white on black · dedication 'For Jack' · specific image of the actual Parsonage in Pasadena on June 18 1952 · fade to black · credits with dedication
sub_zones.notable: Ending 5 · THE CORRECTION · TRUE ending · requires all 6 Corrections and Working IX refused

sub_zones.notable: Ending 6 · HUBBARD TAKES THE CREDIT · worst ending · game tells the player they missed something

compression_notes.notes: How Astro-Cortex compressed 12 manuscript chapters into 6 game chapters
compressions: Manuscript Ch. II (The Passage · 3 pages) → 15 seconds of screen time · Rocha's team decided the ellipsis was in character for Hubbard and let it stand

Manuscript Ch. VII (The Hunt · staged tribal war between Delvanni tribes) → Chapter 4 (The Mines) · Rocha's team recontextualized · the staged war is now known to be by the Order and its goal is Kelait raid
Manuscript Chs. IX-XI (Oracle, Betrayal, Great Work) → Chapter 5 (The Academy) · condensed three chapters into one location · reveal-heavy
Manuscript Ch. XII (Babalon Working) → Chapter 6 (The Finale) · expanded from single climactic chapter to six possible endings

playtime_estimates.completionist_run: 40-50 hours to see all six endings and all six Corrections
